docs.title: AgentSentinel
docs.text:
Auto-approve tool requests for AI coding CLIs running in tmux.

AgentSentinel monitors tmux panes for tool approval prompts from AI coding assistants and automatically responds with y to approve them. Designed for macOS with iTerm2 and tmux.

docs.title: Supported AI CLIs
docs.text: CLI Prompt Format AWS Kiro CLI tool use read requires approval, press 'y' to approve Claude Code Allow? (Y/n) Codex CLI Proceed? (Y/n) Gemini CLI (Y/n) prompts

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Automatic Approval - Detects and approves tool requests without manual intervention
  • Multi-Agent Support - Handles multiple concurrent AI agents across different tmux panes
  • Kiro Multi-Subagent TUI - Special handling for Kiro's navigable approval list
  • Safety First - Blocks dangerous commands like rm -rf, sudo, curl|bash
  • Configurable - Custom patterns, notifications, and statistics tracking
  • Dry Run Mode - Test detection without sending approvals

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ tmux pane 1: kiro                   │
│                                     │
│ > tool use read requires approval,  │
│   press 'y' to approve              │  ← AgentSentinel detects this
│                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│ tmux pane 2: agentsentinel watch -v │
│                                     │
│ INFO prompt detected pane=%1        │
│ INFO approved pane=%1               │  ← Sends 'y' to pane 1
└─────────────────────────────────────┘

docs.title: Development
docs.text: Guide for developing and contributing to AgentSentinel.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Go 1.21+
  • tmux 3.0+
  • golangci-lint (for linting)

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Clone the repository:

git clone https://github.com/plexusone/agentsentinel.git
cd agentsentinel

Build:

go build -o agentsentinel .

Run:

./agentsentinel version

docs.title: Build
docs.text:
# Build binary
go build -o agentsentinel .

# Install to $GOPATH/bin
go install .

docs.title: Test
docs.text:
# Run all tests
go test -v ./...

# Run tests with coverage
go test -cover ./...

# Generate coverage report
go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...
go tool cover -html=coverage.out

docs.title: Lint
docs.text:
# Run linter
golangci-lint run

# Fix auto-fixable issues
golangci-lint run --fix

docs.title: Format
docs.text:
# Format code
gofmt -w .

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
agentsentinel/
├── main.go                 # Entry point
├── cmd/                    # CLI commands
├── internal/               # Internal packages
│   ├── watcher/            # Core watch logic
│   ├── config/             # Configuration
│   ├── detector/           # Pattern detection
│   ├── tmux/               # tmux integration
│   ├── stats/              # Statistics
│   └── notify/             # Notifications
├── docs/                   # Documentation
├── mkdocs.yml              # MkDocs config
├── go.mod                  # Go module
└── go.sum                  # Go dependencies

docs.title: Built-in Patterns
docs.text:
Add patterns in internal/detector/detector.go:

// In NewDetector()
patterns: []*regexp.Regexp{
    regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)your-new-pattern`),
    // ...
}

docs.title: Adding Tests
docs.text:
Add test cases in internal/detector/detector_test.go:

{
    name:     "Your new pattern",
    input:    "Your prompt text (Y/n)",
    wantType: PromptAllow,
    wantLine: "Your prompt text (Y/n)",
}

docs.title: Adding Danger Patterns
docs.text:
In internal/detector/detector.go:

dangerPatterns: []*regexp.Regexp{
    regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)dangerous-command`),
}

docs.title: Adding a New Command
docs.text:
1. Create cmd/newcmd.go:
package cmd

import "github.com/spf13/cobra"

var newCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "newcmd",
    Short: "Description of the command",
    RunE:  runNewCmd,
}

func init() {
    rootCmd.AddCommand(newCmd)
}

func runNewCmd(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // Implementation
    return nil
}
  1. Follow existing command structure (see cmd/status.go for a simple example).

docs.title: Modifying Watch Behavior
docs.text:
Core watch logic is in internal/watcher/watcher.go:

  • watcher.New() - Create watcher with dependencies
  • watcher.Scan(ctx) - Per-tick scan of all panes
  • wasRecentlyApproved() - Duplicate prevention
  • markApproved() - Track approved panes

The cmd/watch.go handles CLI setup and the main ticker loop.

docs.title: Testing Without tmux
docs.text:
The watcher package uses interfaces for dependency injection:

type TmuxClient interface {
    ListPanes() ([]string, error)
    CapturePane(paneID string, lines int) (string, error)
    Approve(paneID string) error
    ApproveMultiple(paneID string, count int, delayMs int) error
}

Create mock implementations for testing:

type mockTmuxClient struct {
    panes        []string
    paneContents map[string]string
}

func (m *mockTmuxClient) ListPanes() ([]string, error) {
    return m.panes, nil
}
// ... implement other methods

See internal/watcher/watcher_test.go for examples.

docs.title: Building Docs
docs.text:
Install MkDocs with Material theme:

pip install mkdocs-material

Serve locally:

mkdocs serve

Build static site:

mkdocs build

docs.title: Updating Architecture Diagram
docs.text:
Edit docs/architecture.d2 and regenerate:

d2 docs/architecture.d2 docs/architecture.svg

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• Follow standard Go conventions
  • Use gofmt for formatting
  • Run golangci-lint before committing
  • Keep functions focused and small
  • Prefer returning errors over logging them

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
Follow this priority:

  1. Return - If the function can return an error
  2. Log - Use context-based logging: slogutil.LoggerFromContext(ctx)
  3. Panic - Only for programming errors / invariant violations

docs.title: Commit Messages
docs.text:
Follow Conventional Commits:

feat(detector): add support for new CLI prompt
fix: resolve race condition in approval tracking
docs: update pattern reference
test: add integration tests for watcher

docs.title: Pull Request Guidelines
docs.text:
1. Create a feature branch from main
  2. Write tests for new functionality
  3. Ensure all tests pass: go test ./...
  4. Ensure linting passes: golangci-lint run
  5. Update documentation if needed
  6. Submit PR with clear description

docs.title: Release Process
docs.text:
1. Update version in code
  2. Update CHANGELOG
  3. Create and push tag: git tag v1.0.0 && git push --tags
  4. GitHub Actions will build and release

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Common issues and solutions when using AgentSentinel.

docs.title: "tmux is not installed"
docs.text:
Install tmux via Homebrew:

brew install tmux

docs.title: "tmux server is not running"
docs.text:
Start a tmux session first:

tmux new-session -s agents

Then run AgentSentinel.

docs.title: Can't see any panes
docs.text:
Check that tmux is running and has panes:

# List all tmux sessions
tmux list-sessions

# List all panes
tmux list-panes -a

If no panes are listed, start a tmux session first.

docs.title: Prompts not being detected
docs.text:
1. Enable verbose logging

    agentsentinel watch -v

  2. Check the prompt is in the visible area

    AgentSentinel captures the last 30 lines by default. If the prompt scrolled up:

    agentsentinel watch --lines 50

  3. Run the test command

    agentsentinel test

  4. Check the exact prompt format

    The prompt may use a different format than expected. Run agentsentinel status and manually inspect the pane content:

    tmux capture-pane -t %1 -p | tail -30

  5. Add a custom pattern

    If the prompt format isn't covered by built-in patterns:

    # ~/.agentsentinel.yaml
patterns:
  - "(?i)my-custom-prompt"

docs.title: Wrong prompts being detected
docs.text:
Use dry-run mode to see what's being detected:

agentsentinel watch --dry-run -v

If unwanted prompts are being matched, the built-in patterns may be too broad for your use case. Consider filing an issue.

docs.title: Approvals not working
docs.text:
1. Verify the pane ID

    agentsentinel status

  2. Test manually

    Send a keystroke directly to verify tmux can interact with the pane:

    tmux send-keys -t %1 y Enter

  3. Check CLI focus

    Some CLIs may require focus or specific terminal state. Ensure the CLI is waiting for input.

  4. Check for duplicate prevention

    If you recently approved the same pane, AgentSentinel waits 5 seconds before re-approving. Wait and try again.

docs.title: Double approvals
docs.text:
This shouldn't happen with the default duplicate prevention. If it does:

  1. Check if you're running multiple AgentSentinel instances
  2. Increase the scan interval: --interval 2s

docs.title: Config file not loading
docs.text:
1. Check the path

    agentsentinel config path

  2. Verify YAML syntax

    agentsentinel config show

    If this errors, there's a syntax issue in your config file.

  3. Common YAML mistakes

    • Missing spaces after colons: interval:1s → interval: 1s
    • Incorrect indentation (use spaces, not tabs)
    • Unescaped special characters in patterns

docs.title: Custom patterns not working
docs.text:
1. Check YAML escaping

    Backslashes must be doubled in YAML:

    patterns:
  - "(?i)deploy\\s+to\\s+production\\?"  # Correct
  - "(?i)deploy\s+to\s+production\?"     # Wrong

  2. Test the pattern

    agentsentinel test

  3. Use dry-run mode

    agentsentinel watch --dry-run -v

docs.title: High CPU usage
docs.text:
Reduce scan frequency:

agentsentinel watch --interval 2s

docs.title: Slow response time
docs.text:
If approvals seem delayed:

  1. Decrease scan interval: --interval 500ms
  2. Reduce lines captured: --lines 20

docs.title: macOS notifications not appearing
docs.text:
1. Check System Preferences

    Go to System Preferences → Notifications and ensure "Script Editor" or terminal app has notification permissions.

  2. Test osascript directly

    osascript -e 'display notification "Test" with title "AgentSentinel"'

  3. Check Do Not Disturb

    Ensure Do Not Disturb is not enabled.

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
If none of these solutions work:

  1. Check existing issues

    GitHub Issues

  2. File a new issue

    Include:

    • AgentSentinel version: agentsentinel version
    • macOS version
    • tmux version: tmux -V
    • Verbose log output: agentsentinel watch -v
    • The exact prompt text you're trying to detect

docs.title: Option 1: Install from Go (Recommended)
docs.text:
Install directly to your $GOPATH/bin:

go install github.com/plexusone/agentsentinel@latest

Make sure ~/go/bin is in your PATH:

# Add to ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc
export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$PATH"

Verify the installation:

agentsentinel version

docs.title: Option 2: Build from Source
docs.text:
Clone and build locally:

git clone https://github.com/plexusone/agentsentinel.git
cd agentsentinel
go build -o agentsentinel .

Run from the current directory:

./agentsentinel version

Or move to a directory in your PATH:

mv agentsentinel /usr/local/bin/

docs.title: Shell Completion
docs.text:
Enable tab completion for your shell:

BashZshFish 
agentsentinel completion bash > /usr/local/etc/bash_completion.d/agentsentinel

agentsentinel completion zsh > "${fpath[1]}/_agentsentinel"

agentsentinel completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/agentsentinel.fish

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
Check that everything is working:

# Check version
agentsentinel version

# Check tmux connectivity
agentsentinel status

If agentsentinel status shows tmux information, you're ready to go.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: • Quick Start - Start monitoring AI CLIs

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This guide gets you from zero to auto-approving AI tool requests in minutes.

docs.title: Step 1: Start tmux
docs.text:
Open iTerm2 and start a tmux session:

tmux new-session -s agents

You're now inside tmux.

docs.title: Step 2: Run Your AI CLI
docs.text:
Inside the tmux session, start your AI coding assistant:

# AWS Kiro
kiro

# Or Claude Code
claude

# Or Codex CLI
codex

docs.title: Step 3: Run AgentSentinel
docs.text: You have three options for where to run AgentSentinel:

docs.title: Option A: In Another tmux Pane (Recommended)
docs.text:
Split the tmux window and run AgentSentinel in the new pane:

# Split horizontally
# Press: Ctrl+b "

# Or split vertically
# Press: Ctrl+b %

# In the new pane, run:
agentsentinel watch -v

docs.title: Option B: In a Separate iTerm2 Tab
docs.text:
Open a new iTerm2 tab (Cmd+T) and run AgentSentinel directly (not in tmux):

agentsentinel watch -v

docs.title: Option C: Background tmux Session
docs.text:
Create a detached session for AgentSentinel:

tmux new-session -d -s sentinel 'agentsentinel watch -v'

docs.title: Step 4: Watch It Work
docs.text:
Once running, AgentSentinel monitors all tmux panes and auto-approves tool requests:

┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ tmux pane 1: kiro                   │
│                                     │
│ > tool use read requires approval,  │
│   press 'y' to approve              │  ← Detected
│                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│ tmux pane 2: agentsentinel watch -v │
│                                     │
│ INFO prompt detected pane=%1        │
│ INFO approved pane=%1               │  ← Approved
└─────────────────────────────────────┘

docs.title: Key Concept
docs.text:
AgentSentinel monitors tmux from the outside

AgentSentinel uses tmux commands to read pane contents and send keystrokes. It doesn't run "inside" your AI CLI. It runs separately and watches all tmux panes.

docs.title: Quick Test
docs.text:
Before relying on AgentSentinel, test it:

# Check AgentSentinel can see your tmux panes
agentsentinel status

# Dry run to see what it would approve (without actually approving)
agentsentinel watch --dry-run -v

docs.title: Where to Run AgentSentinel
docs.text: Location Pros Cons tmux pane See logs alongside agents Uses a pane Separate iTerm2 tab Doesn't use tmux space Switch tabs to see logs Background session Out of the way Need to attach to see logs

docs.text:
• Commands - Full CLI reference
  • Configuration - Customize behavior
  • Multi-Agent Workflows - Run multiple AI agents

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text: Requirement Version Notes macOS 10.15+ Primary supported platform tmux 3.0+ Terminal multiplexer Go 1.21+ Only needed for building from source

docs.title: Installing tmux
docs.text:
If tmux is not already installed:

brew install tmux

Verify the installation:

tmux -V
# tmux 3.4

docs.title: tmux Basics
docs.text: If you're new to tmux, here are the essential concepts:

docs.title: Sessions, Windows, and Panes
docs.text:
• Session - A collection of windows (like a workspace)
  • Window - A full-screen view within a session (like a tab)
  • Pane - A subdivision of a window (split view)

docs.title: Essential Commands
docs.text: Action Command Start new session tmux new-session -s name List sessions tmux list-sessions Attach to session tmux attach -t name Detach from session Ctrl+b d Split horizontally Ctrl+b " Split vertically Ctrl+b % Switch panes Ctrl+b arrow-key

docs.title: Why tmux?
docs.text:
AgentSentinel works by:

  1. Listing all tmux panes via tmux list-panes
  2. Reading pane content via tmux capture-pane
  3. Sending keystrokes via tmux send-keys

This means your AI CLI must run inside a tmux pane for AgentSentinel to monitor and interact with it.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Installation - Install AgentSentinel
  • Quick Start - Get up and running

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: AgentSentinel is built with a modular architecture that separates concerns into testable packages.

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text:
1. Pane Discovery - Lists all tmux panes via tmux list-panes
  2. Content Capture - Reads each pane's visible content via tmux capture-pane
  3. Pattern Matching - Scans for approval prompts using regex patterns
  4. Danger Check - Verifies the command isn't dangerous
  5. Keystroke Injection - Sends y + Enter via tmux send-keys

docs.title: Package Structure
docs.text:
agentsentinel/
├── main.go                 # Entry point
├── cmd/                    # CLI commands (Cobra)
│   ├── root.go             # Root command, --verbose flag
│   ├── watch.go            # Watch command setup and main loop
│   ├── status.go           # Show tmux status
│   ├── config.go           # Configuration management
│   ├── test.go             # Pattern detection tests
│   ├── stats.go            # Statistics display
│   └── version.go          # Version info
└── internal/
    ├── watcher/            # Core watch logic
    │   ├── watcher.go      # Watcher struct, Scan(), interfaces
    │   └── watcher_test.go
    ├── config/             # YAML configuration
    │   ├── config.go       # Load/save ~/.agentsentinel.yaml
    │   └── config_test.go
    ├── detector/           # Prompt detection
    │   ├── detector.go     # Regex pattern matching
    │   └── detector_test.go
    ├── tmux/               # tmux integration
    │   ├── tmux.go         # Pane listing, capture, send-keys
    │   └── tmux_test.go
    ├── stats/              # Statistics tracking
    │   ├── stats.go        # Approval counters, JSON logging
    │   └── stats_test.go
    └── notify/             # macOS notifications
        ├── notify.go       # osascript wrapper
        └── notify_test.go

docs.title: Key Interfaces
docs.text: The internal/watcher package defines interfaces for dependency injection, enabling unit testing without real tmux:

docs.title: TmuxClient
docs.text:
type TmuxClient interface {
    ListPanes() ([]string, error)
    CapturePane(paneID string, lines int) (string, error)
    Approve(paneID string) error
    ApproveMultiple(paneID string, count int, delayMs int) error
}

Implemented by internal/tmux.Client.

docs.title: Notifier
docs.text:
type Notifier interface {
    NotifyApproval(paneID, promptType string) error
    NotifyBlocked(paneID string) error
}

Implemented by internal/notify.Notifier.

docs.title: StatsRecorder
docs.text:
type StatsRecorder interface {
    RecordScan()
    RecordApproval(ctx context.Context, paneID, promptType, line string, blocked bool)
}

Implemented by internal/stats.Stats.

docs.title: Watch Loop
docs.text:
The main watch loop in cmd/watch.go:

ticker := time.NewTicker(watchInterval)
for {
    select {
    case <-ticker.C:
        if err := watcher.Scan(ctx); err != nil {
            logger.Error("scan error", "error", err)
        }
    case sig := <-sigChan:
        // Graceful shutdown
        return nil
    }
}

Each Scan() call:

  1. Lists all tmux panes
  2. Skips recently approved panes
  3. Captures content from each pane
  4. Runs detection patterns
  5. Checks for dangerous commands
  6. Sends approval if safe

docs.title: Kiro Multi-Subagent Handling
docs.text:
For Kiro's multi-subagent TUI:

if detector.IsKiroPrompt(detection.Line) {
    cycleCount := max(detection.Count, 4)
    client.ApproveMultiple(paneID, cycleCount, 100)
}

ApproveMultiple sends y followed by j (navigate down) to cycle through all pending approvals.

docs.title: Context-Based Logging
docs.text:
Logger is propagated via context.Context:

import "github.com/grokify/mogo/log/slogutil"

// Create context with logger
ctx := slogutil.ContextWithLogger(context.Background(), logger)

// Retrieve in functions
func doWork(ctx context.Context) {
    logger := slogutil.LoggerFromContext(ctx, slog.Default())
    logger.Info("working", "key", value)
}

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: Package Purpose github.com/spf13/cobra CLI framework gopkg.in/yaml.v3 YAML configuration github.com/lmittmann/tint Colored slog output github.com/grokify/mogo Context-based logging utilities

docs.title: Test Coverage
docs.text: Package Coverage internal/watcher 85.3% internal/detector 88.9% internal/stats 88.7% internal/config 73.9% internal/tmux 53.4% internal/notify 50.0%

docs.title: D2 Diagram Source
docs.text:
The architecture diagram is generated from D2:

# Render SVG from D2 source
d2 docs/architecture.d2 docs/architecture.svg

Source: docs/architecture.d2

docs.title: Pattern Reference
docs.text: AgentSentinel uses regex patterns to detect approval prompts and dangerous commands.

docs.title: Built-in Detection Patterns
docs.text: AgentSentinel includes 19 built-in regex patterns for detecting approval prompts:

docs.title: Generic Y/N Patterns
docs.text: Pattern Matches (?i)\(y/n\)\s*$ Allow? (Y/n) (?i)\[y/n\]\s*$ Continue? [y/n] (?i)\(yes/no\)\s*$ Confirm? (yes/no) (?i)\[yes/no\]\s*$ Proceed? [yes/no]

docs.title: Tool Request Patterns
docs.text: Pattern Matches (?i)allow\s*\?\s*\(y/n\) Allow? (Y/n) (?i)allow\s+tool Allow tool execution (?i)tool\s+request Tool request pending (?i)approve\s+tool Approve tool use (?i)proceed\s*\? Proceed? (?i)continue\s*\?\s*\(y/n\) Continue? (Y/n) (?i)execute\s*\?\s*\(y/n\) Execute? (Y/n) (?i)run\s+command\s*\? Run command?

docs.title: Claude Code Patterns
docs.text: Pattern Matches (?i)allow\s+once Allow once (?i)allow\s+always Allow always

docs.title: Codex CLI Patterns
docs.text: Pattern Matches (?i)sandbox\s+execution Sandbox execution

docs.title: AWS Kiro Patterns
docs.text: Pattern Matches (?i)tool\s+use\s+\w+\s+requires\s+approval tool use read requires approval (?i)press\s+'y'\s+to\s+approve press 'y' to approve

docs.title: Generic Patterns
docs.text: Pattern Matches (?i)permission\s+required Permission required (?i)confirm\s+action Confirm action

docs.title: Built-in Danger Patterns
docs.text: These patterns block auto-approval when detected (13 patterns):

docs.title: Destructive Commands
docs.text: Pattern Blocks (?i)rm\s+-rf rm -rf /path (?i)rm\s+-r\s+/ rm -r / (?i)sudo\s+rm sudo rm -rf (?i)mkfs mkfs.ext4 /dev/sda (?i)dd\s+if= dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/sda

docs.title: Fork Bomb
docs.text: Pattern Blocks (?i):\(\)\s*\{\s*:\|:\s*&\s*\} :(){ :|:& };:

docs.title: Device Write
docs.text: Pattern Blocks (?i)>\s*/dev/sd > /dev/sda

docs.title: Permission Changes
docs.text: Pattern Blocks (?i)chmod\s+-R\s+777\s+/ chmod -R 777 / (?i)chown\s+-R.*\s+/ chown -R root /

docs.title: Remote Code Execution
docs.text: Pattern Blocks (?i)curl.*\|\s*sh curl url \| sh (?i)curl.*\|\s*bash curl url \| bash (?i)wget.*\|\s*sh wget url \| sh (?i)wget.*\|\s*bash wget url \| bash

docs.title: Custom Patterns
docs.text:
Add custom patterns in ~/.agentsentinel.yaml:

# Custom approval patterns
patterns:
  - "(?i)deploy\\s+to\\s+production\\?"
  - "(?i)apply\\s+changes\\?"
  - "my-tool-prompt"

# Custom danger patterns
danger_patterns:
  - "(?i)drop\\s+database"
  - "(?i)truncate\\s+table"
  - "(?i)delete\\s+from.*where\\s+1=1"

docs.title: Pattern Syntax
docs.text: Patterns use Go's regexp package (RE2 syntax).

docs.title: Common Syntax
docs.text: Syntax Meaning Example (?i) Case-insensitive (?i)allow matches Allow, ALLOW, allow \s+ One or more whitespace tool\s+use matches tool use, tool use \s* Zero or more whitespace allow\s*\? matches allow?, allow ? $ End of line \(y/n\)$ only matches at line end \w+ One or more word chars tool\s+\w+ matches tool use, tool read .* Any characters curl.*bash matches curl http://x \| bash \| Literal pipe \|\s*sh matches \| sh

docs.title: Escaping Special Characters
docs.text: Character Escaped Example ? \? proceed\? ( ) \( \) \(y/n\) . \. file\.txt | \| curl\|bash [ ] \[ \] \[yes/no\]

docs.title: YAML Escaping
docs.text:
Double backslashes in YAML

In YAML strings, backslashes must be doubled:

patterns:
  - "(?i)deploy\\s+to\\s+production\\?"

docs.title: Using the Test Command
docs.text:
agentsentinel test

This runs detection against sample prompts and shows which patterns match.

docs.title: Using Dry-Run Mode
docs.text:
agentsentinel watch --dry-run -v

This detects prompts but doesn't send approvals, letting you see what would be approved.

docs.title: Manual Testing
docs.text:
Test a regex pattern with Go:

package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "regexp"
)

func main() {
    pattern := regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)deploy\s+to\s+production\?`)
    text := "Deploy to production? (y/n)"
    fmt.Println(pattern.MatchString(text)) // true
}

docs.title: Safety Features
docs.text: AgentSentinel includes several safety features to prevent accidental approval of dangerous commands.

docs.title: Dangerous Command Blocking
docs.text: By default, AgentSentinel blocks auto-approval for commands that could cause significant damage.

docs.title: What Gets Blocked
docs.text: Category Commands File deletion rm -rf, rm -r /, sudo rm Disk operations mkfs, dd if= Fork bomb :(){ :\|:& };: Device write > /dev/sda Permission changes chmod -R 777 /, chown -R ... / Remote execution curl \| sh, curl \| bash, wget \| sh, wget \| bash

docs.title: When Blocking Occurs
docs.text:
When a dangerous command is detected:

  1. The prompt is not approved
  2. A warning is logged
  3. If notifications are enabled, you receive an alert
  4. The approval is recorded as "blocked" in stats
WARN dangerous command detected, skipping auto-approval pane=%3

docs.title: Disabling Danger Blocking
docs.text:
Not Recommended

Disabling danger blocking removes an important safety net. Only disable if you understand the risks.

Via CLI flag:

agentsentinel watch --block-danger=false

Via config:

watch:
  block_danger: false

docs.title: Dry Run Mode
docs.text:
Test AgentSentinel without actually sending approvals:

agentsentinel watch --dry-run -v

In dry-run mode:

  • Prompts are detected and logged
  • Dangerous commands are flagged
  • No keystrokes are sent to tmux

This is perfect for:

  • Testing custom patterns
  • Verifying detection works correctly
  • Auditing what would be approved

docs.title: Example Output
docs.text:
INFO prompt detected pane=%1 type=Allow line="Allow? (Y/n)" blocked=false
INFO dry run: would approve pane=%1 count=1

docs.title: Duplicate Prevention
docs.text: AgentSentinel tracks recently approved panes to prevent sending multiple approvals to the same prompt.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
• After approving a pane, it's marked as "recently approved"
  • For the next 5 seconds, that pane won't receive another approval
  • This prevents double-approvals when the prompt hasn't cleared yet

docs.title: Why This Matters
docs.text:
Without duplicate prevention:

  1. AgentSentinel detects a prompt and sends y
  2. The AI CLI takes 500ms to process the approval
  3. AgentSentinel scans again, sees the same prompt, sends another y
  4. The extra y might approve something unintended

docs.title: Custom Danger Patterns
docs.text:
Add your own patterns to block:

danger_patterns:
  - "(?i)drop\\s+database"
  - "(?i)truncate\\s+table"
  - "(?i)delete\\s+from.*where\\s+1=1"
  - "(?i)format\\s+c:"
  - "(?i)shutdown\\s+-h"

See Pattern Reference for syntax details.

docs.title: Notifications
docs.text:
Get alerted when dangerous commands are blocked:

agentsentinel watch --notify

Or in config:

notifications:
  enabled: true
  sound: true

You'll receive a macOS notification when:

  • A prompt is approved
  • A dangerous command is blocked

docs.title: Statistics
docs.text:
Track blocked commands with stats:

agentsentinel watch --stats

On shutdown, you'll see blocked count:

AgentSentinel Statistics
========================
Total approvals: 15
Blocked (dangerous): 2

Enable logging to file for audit purposes:

stats:
  enabled: true
  log_file: ~/agentsentinel-approvals.log

Each approval is logged as JSON:

{"timestamp":"2024-01-15T10:30:45Z","pane_id":"%1","type":"Allow","line":"Allow? (Y/n)","blocked":false}
{"timestamp":"2024-01-15T10:31:02Z","pane_id":"%2","type":"Allow","line":"rm -rf / (Y/n)","blocked":true}

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Always test with --dry-run first - Verify detection works before going live

  2. Keep --block-danger enabled - The default is there for a reason

  3. Review custom patterns carefully - Overly broad patterns might match unintended prompts

  4. Use stats logging for audit - Track what's being approved for later review

  5. Enable notifications - Stay aware of blocked commands

  6. Monitor specific sessions - Use --session to limit scope when testing

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: AgentSentinel provides several commands for monitoring, testing, and configuration.

docs.title: watch
docs.text:
Monitor tmux panes and auto-approve tool requests.

agentsentinel watch [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Short Default Description --interval -i 1s Scan interval --session -s "" Watch specific tmux session only --dry-run false Detect but don't approve --block-danger true Block dangerous commands --lines 30 Lines to capture per pane --stats false Enable statistics tracking --notify false Enable macOS notifications --level -l info Log level (debug, info, warn, error) --verbose -v false Verbose output (same as --level debug)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Watch all tmux panes
agentsentinel watch

# Watch with verbose logging
agentsentinel watch -v

# Watch a specific session
agentsentinel watch --session my-coding-session

# Faster scanning (500ms interval)
agentsentinel watch --interval 500ms

# Dry run mode (detect but don't approve)
agentsentinel watch --dry-run -v

# Enable stats and notifications
agentsentinel watch --stats --notify

# Set log level
agentsentinel watch --level debug

docs.title: status
docs.text:
Show tmux environment and available panes.

agentsentinel status

docs.title: Example Output
docs.text:
AgentSentinel Status
====================

tmux: installed
tmux server: running
current session: dev

Panes (4 total):

  %1 (session: $0, window: @0, index: 0)
  %2 (session: $0, window: @0, index: 1)
  %3 (session: $0, window: @1, index: 0)
  %4 (session: $1, window: @2, index: 0)

Run 'agentsentinel watch' to start monitoring for tool prompts.

docs.title: test
docs.text:
Verify prompt detection is working with sample prompts.

agentsentinel test

This runs detection against sample prompts and shows which patterns match.

docs.title: config
docs.text:
Manage the configuration file.

agentsentinel config <subcommand>

docs.title: Subcommands
docs.text: Subcommand Description path Show config file path show Show current configuration init Create example config file example Print example configuration

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Show config file path
agentsentinel config path
# ~/.agentsentinel.yaml

# Show current configuration
agentsentinel config show

# Create example config file
agentsentinel config init

# Print example configuration to stdout
agentsentinel config example

docs.title: version
docs.text:
Display version information.

agentsentinel version

docs.title: completion
docs.text:
Generate shell completion scripts.

agentsentinel completion <shell>

docs.title: Supported Shells
docs.text:
• bash
  • zsh
  • fish
  • powershell

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Bash
agentsentinel completion bash > /usr/local/etc/bash_completion.d/agentsentinel

# Zsh
agentsentinel completion zsh > "${fpath[1]}/_agentsentinel"

# Fish
agentsentinel completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/agentsentinel.fish

docs.title: Global Flags
docs.text:
These flags work with all commands:

Flag Short Description --help -h Show help for command --verbose -v Enable verbose output

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: AgentSentinel can be configured via a YAML file at ~/.agentsentinel.yaml.

docs.title: Location
docs.text:
The default configuration path is:

~/.agentsentinel.yaml

Check the path with:

agentsentinel config path

docs.title: Creating a Config File
docs.text:
Generate an example configuration:

agentsentinel config init

Or print the example to stdout:

agentsentinel config example

docs.title: Full Example
docs.text:
# Watch settings
watch:
  interval: 1s          # Scan interval (default: 1s)
  session: ""           # Filter to specific tmux session (default: all)
  lines: 30             # Lines to capture per pane (default: 30)
  block_danger: true    # Block dangerous commands (default: true)

# Custom detection patterns (Go regex syntax)
patterns:
  - "(?i)my-custom-prompt"
  - "(?i)deploy\\s+to\\s+production\\?"

# Custom danger patterns (Go regex syntax)
danger_patterns:
  - "(?i)drop\\s+database"
  - "(?i)truncate\\s+table"

# macOS notification settings
notifications:
  enabled: false        # Enable notifications (default: false)
  sound: true           # Play sound with notifications (default: true)
  title: "AgentSentinel"  # Notification title

# Statistics tracking
stats:
  enabled: false        # Enable stats tracking (default: false)
  log_file: ""          # JSON log file path (optional)

docs.title: Watch Settings
docs.text: Option Type Default Description interval duration 1s How often to scan tmux panes session string "" Limit to specific tmux session lines int 30 Lines to capture from each pane block_danger bool true Block dangerous commands

docs.title: Custom Patterns
docs.text:
Add patterns to detect prompts not covered by built-in patterns:

patterns:
  - "(?i)deploy\\s+to\\s+production\\?"
  - "(?i)apply\\s+changes\\?"
  - "my-custom-tool-prompt"

docs.title: Custom Danger Patterns
docs.text:
Add patterns to block from auto-approval:

danger_patterns:
  - "(?i)drop\\s+database"
  - "(?i)truncate\\s+table"
  - "(?i)delete\\s+from.*where\\s+1=1"

docs.title: Notifications
docs.text:
Enable macOS notifications for approvals:

notifications:
  enabled: true
  sound: true
  title: "AgentSentinel"

docs.title: Statistics
docs.text:
Track approval statistics:

stats:
  enabled: true
  log_file: ~/agentsentinel-approvals.log

When enabled, stats are printed on shutdown (Ctrl+C). The log file records approvals in JSON format.

docs.title: CLI Flag Precedence
docs.text:
CLI flags override configuration file values:

# Config file has interval: 1s
# This overrides to 500ms
agentsentinel watch --interval 500ms

docs.title: Pattern Syntax
docs.text:
Patterns use Go's regexp package (RE2 syntax):

Syntax Meaning (?i) Case-insensitive matching \s+ One or more whitespace characters \s* Zero or more whitespace characters $ End of line anchor \w+ One or more word characters \? Literal question mark \( \) Literal parentheses

docs.title: Example Patterns
docs.text:
patterns:
  # Match "Deploy to production?" (case-insensitive)
  - "(?i)deploy\\s+to\\s+production\\?"

  # Match "Apply changes? [y/n]"
  - "(?i)apply\\s+changes\\?\\s*\\[y/n\\]"

  # Match exact string
  - "my-tool: confirm action"

Escaping in YAML

In YAML, backslashes must be doubled: \\s instead of \s

docs.title: Testing Configuration
docs.text:
Test your patterns:

# Run the test command
agentsentinel test

# Or use dry-run with verbose output
agentsentinel watch --dry-run -v

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Pattern Reference - Built-in patterns
  • Safety Features - Danger pattern details

docs.title: Multi-Agent Workflows
docs.text: AgentSentinel handles multiple concurrent AI agents across different tmux panes, including special support for Kiro's multi-subagent TUI.

docs.title: Multiple Panes
docs.text:
Run different AI agents in separate tmux panes:

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ iTerm2                                                 │
│ ┌─────────────────────┬─────────────────────┐          │
│ │ tmux pane 1         │ tmux pane 2         │          │
│ │ $ kiro              │ $ claude            │          │
│ │                     │                     │          │
│ │ Agent working...    │ Agent working...    │          │
│ │ tool use requires   │ Allow? (Y/n)        │          │
│ │ approval, press 'y' │        ↑            │          │
│ │        ↑            │        │            │          │
│ │        │            │        │            │          │
│ │        └────────────┴────────┘            │          │
│ │                     │                     │          │
│ ├─────────────────────┴─────────────────────┤          │
│ │ tmux pane 3                               │          │
│ │ $ agentsentinel watch -v                  │          │
│ │ INFO starting watcher session="all"       │          │
│ │ INFO prompt detected pane=%1 type=Approve │          │
│ │ INFO approved pane=%1                     │          │
│ └───────────────────────────────────────────┘          │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

AgentSentinel scans all panes on each interval, detecting and approving prompts in any pane.

docs.title: Kiro Multi-Subagent TUI
docs.text:
AWS Kiro can run multiple subagents concurrently (typically 4). These appear in a navigable TUI list within a single pane:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ kiro                                        │
│                                             │
│ ↳ tool use read requires approval,         │
│   press 'y' to approve, 'n' to deny...     │
│ ↳ tool use write requires approval,        │
│   press 'y' to approve, 'n' to deny...     │
│ ↳ tool use execute requires approval,      │
│   press 'y' to approve, 'n' to deny...     │
│ ↳ tool use read requires approval,         │
│   press 'y' to approve, 'n' to deny...     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘

docs.title: How AgentSentinel Handles This
docs.text:
When AgentSentinel detects Kiro's multi-subagent format:

  1. Counts pending prompts - Identifies how many tool use ... requires approval lines exist
  2. Cycles through all items - Sends y (approve) + j (navigate down) repeatedly
  3. Full coverage - Performs count+1 iterations to ensure all items are approved regardless of cursor starting position
# Example log output
INFO kiro multi-subagent detected, cycling through all pane=%1 detected=4 cycles=4
INFO approved pane=%1 count=4

docs.title: Session Filtering
docs.text:
If you have multiple tmux sessions, you can limit monitoring to a specific one:

# Watch only the "agents" session
agentsentinel watch --session agents

Or in config:

watch:
  session: "agents"

docs.title: Faster Scanning
docs.text:
For responsive approval with multiple agents, reduce the scan interval:

# Scan every 500ms
agentsentinel watch --interval 500ms

docs.title: Background with Logging
docs.text:
Run AgentSentinel in the background while working:

# Background with logging
agentsentinel watch -v > ~/agentsentinel.log 2>&1 &

# Check logs
tail -f ~/agentsentinel.log

# Stop it
pkill agentsentinel

Or use a dedicated tmux session:

# Create detached session
tmux new-session -d -s sentinel 'agentsentinel watch -v'

# Attach to check logs
tmux attach -t sentinel

# Detach again
# Press: Ctrl+b d

docs.title: Duplicate Prevention
docs.text:
AgentSentinel tracks recently approved panes to prevent sending multiple approvals to the same prompt. By default, it won't re-approve the same pane within 5 seconds.

This prevents issues when:

  • The prompt hasn't cleared yet after approval
  • Multiple scan cycles occur before the AI CLI processes the approval

docs.title: Statistics for Multiple Agents
docs.text:
Enable stats to track approvals across all agents:

agentsentinel watch --stats

On shutdown (Ctrl+C), you'll see:

AgentSentinel Statistics
========================
Total scans: 120
Total approvals: 15
Blocked (dangerous): 0

Approvals by pane:
  %1: 8
  %2: 5
  %3: 2

Approvals by type:
  Approve: 8
  Allow: 7
